US Existing-Home Sales Rise to Fastest Pace in Seven Months
data.description:
US contract closings increased 1.5% to an annual rate of 4.06 million in September, the highest in seven months, according to National Association of Realtors figures released Thursday. That matched the median estimate of economists surveyed by Bloomberg. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest."

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Output at North Sea Ekofisk 2/4 K platform is shut, TV2 reports
Toronto Stocks Rally; FirstService Shares Down on Weaker Restoration, Roofing Business in 3Q
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


Toronto's indexes continued the rally mid-trading on Thursday after a gold-price-led decline earlier in the week. Most sectors were trending positively in the session, led by process industries, energy and tech stocks. These offset declines in consumer services, transportation, consumer discretionary and utilities.

Manufacturing activity in Canada rebounded last month, rising an estimated 2.8% thanks to a jump in sales as factories show signs of recovering from the uncertainty of U.S. tariffs.

On the retail front, retail sales in Canada rose 1% in August, led by increased trade at motor-vehicle-and-parts dealers.

Canada's S&P/TSX Composite Index rose 0.7% to 301177.38 and the blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 advanced by 0.6% to 1778.73.

Shares in FirstService fell by 5.9% to 242.86 Canadian dollars after the residential-property-management company reported weakness in its restoration and roofing activity in the third quarter, which dragged on profit.


Other market movers:

Dye & Durham shares fell 17% to C$4.33 after the company said its financial adviser resigned from overseeing its strategic review as the company deals with a standoff with activist shareholder Plantro.

Rogers Communications logged a higher profit in the third quarter thanks to the addition of new customers to its mobile and internet services, which gave a boost to revenue growth. Shares rose 0.9% to C$52.74.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1225ET

data.title: DBRS Upgrades Intesa Sanpaolo's Long-Term Rating
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Morningstar DBRS announced on Thursday that it has upgraded Intesa Sanpaolo Spa's senior preferred (unsecured) long-term rating to 'A (low)' from 'BBB (high)', with a Stable trend.

The short-term rating was confirmed at 'R-1 (low)', also with a Stable outlook.

This action follows a similar move regarding Italy, which was announced by the rating agency last Friday.

As the banking group explained, no significant economic or equity effects are expected for the bank.

Intesa Sanpaolo shares closed unchanged on Thursday at EUR5.45 per share.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: S&P cuts India's Tata Motors PV outlook on slow JLR recovery from cyberattack
data.description:
(Reuters) -Ratings agency S&P Global on Thursday downgraded its outlook of India's Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles to 'negative', citing a "slow recovery" of the automaker's key Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) unit, which was hit by a cyberattack early last month.

The cyber incident at UK-based JLR, the cash cow unit of Tata Motors, led to an over three-week pause in production and hurt its sales. The carmaker resumed phased manufacturing late in September.

After the split of Tata Motors into separate firms focused on passenger and commercial vehicles, the impact of JLR's loss of volumes is "more pronounced", S&P said, as it expects JLR to account for more than 80% of Tata Motors PV's earnings going forward.

"Path of recovery remains uncertain. We believe a recovery in JLR's earnings is subject to a series of uncertainties both related to market conditions and to the consequences of the cyberattack."

S&P also downgraded JLR's outlook to 'negative' and said that there could be bigger implications from the attack which are not currently factored into its estimates, such as increased legal costs, impact of brand reputation and delays in new model launches.

While JLR has resumed production, the ramp up to full capacity will likely be gradual, S&P said adding that U.S. tariff-related headwinds and launch delays could pose further risks.

(Reporting by Hritam Mukherjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber and Maju Samuel)

data.title: Einfochips and Nxp Semiconductors Announce Collaboration Focused on Global Distribution and Customer Services
data.description: eInfochips and NXP Semiconductors announced a multi-year collaboration focused on software distribution and customer services. eInfochips will supply standard and premium software packages and tools for NXP's S32 family of microcontrollers and microprocessors to customers. eInfochips will also provide software support and maintenance services for customers, helping them to develop their applications successfully and efficiently in minimum time.

DBRS upgrades Banco BPM's deposit rating to category A
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Morningstar DBRS raised Banco BPM Spa's rating again on Thursday, after increasing its long-term issuer rating by one notch in April.

This time, the agency also raised the bank's long-term deposit rating, improving it from 'BBB (high)' to 'A (low)', with the outlook now Stable from Positive.

The short-term deposit rating was confirmed at 'R-1 (low)', with a Stable outlook.

This rating action reflects Italy's rating upgrade to 'A (low)' last Friday and brings Banco BPM's deposit rating into the 'A' category for the first time.

Banco BPM shares closed Thursday up 0.9% at EUR12.16 per share.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: ADP says consolidated revenue for first nine months of 2025 totalled EUR 5.04 billion
FTSE 100 ends at record high, boosted by oil and earnings
data.description:
(Reuters) -London's FTSE 100 index closed at a record high on Thursday, lifted by oil stocks and positive results from blue-chip firms such as the London Stock Exchange Group and Rentokil.

The commodity-heavy FTSE 100 index rose as much as 0.8% to a record high of 9,594.82 points during the session, before ending up 0.7%.

Oil majors Shell and BP gained 3.2% and 3.8% respectively, as crude prices rose nearly 5% after the U.S. imposed sanctions on major Russian suppliers Rosneft and Lukoil over the Ukraine war. [O/R]

On the earnings front, the London Stock Exchange Group jumped 6.7% after the exchange operator said it would sell 20% of its post-trade services business, announced a surprise 1 billion pound ($1.34 billion) buyback and reported better-than-expected third-quarter results.

Rentokil jumped 9.8% after the pest control firm reported a better-than-expected rise in quarterly organic revenue.

A largely upbeat earnings season, a jump in commodity stocks and signs of cooling inflation that supported bets of a Bank of England interest rate cut have supported UK equity markets this week. The FTSE 100 is on track for its best weekly performance since mid-April.

The FTSE midcap index of domestically oriented stocks rose 0.6%, ending at a near four-year high.

An index of UK precious metal miners advanced 3.7%, tracking gold prices, as U.S. sanctions against Russia and possible new export controls on China stoked geopolitical concerns, buoying demand for safe-haven assets. [GOL/]

On the downside, Holiday Inn owner InterContinental Hotels dipped 1.5% as slowing growth in U.S. markets overshadowed a rise in a key revenue metric. 

Molten Ventures jumped 14.6% to the top of the FTSE 250 after the VC firm forecast 7.2% growth in its net asset value.

AJ Bell fell 2.4% to the bottom of FTSE 250 after the investment platform reported outflows of 1.5 billion pounds ($2.01 billion) at the end of September in its advised platform segment, resulting in lower sequential net inflows.

(Reporting by Avinash P in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber and Andrew Heavens)

data.title: ESA says it will study broad impact of satellite merger
Westamerica Bancorporation Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.46 a Share, Payable Nov. 14 to Shareholders of Record Nov. 3
Vinci's Q3 Revenue Jumps; FY25 Outlook Confirmed
Vinci Backs 2025 Views as Sales Rises Across Segments
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby


Vinci said it is confident of meeting its guidance for the year after sales increased across business areas in the most recent quarter.

The French contractor said Thursday that revenue grew 3.5% on year on a like-for-like basis in the three months through September, reaching 19.4 billion euros ($22.52 billion). That was supported by growth across the concessions, energy-solutions and constructions segments, Vinci said.

The group expects to meets its guidance for the full year of higher sales and earnings than in 2024, before factoring in corporate-tax increases in France.


Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1208ET
